title: USMS Arrest Procedures Policy Directive{s}

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: USMS Arrest Procedures Policy Directive{s}
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The USMS Policy Directive(s) concerning arrest procedures and what is supposed to  happen after the USMS makes an arrest without a warrant.  I have a copy of a document dated 7/24/08 entitled   "USMS  Directives " "8.3 arrests",  however, it is not published on the DOJ websites  nor are any documents about arrest procedures that date from before or after July 24, 2008.  Please send all versions of this document.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kay Sieverding

communications.subject: FOIA Request Number 2020USMS35496
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

I hope this email finds you safe and in good health.

Attached is an DOJ 361 letter and DOJ 361 form that needs to be signed pertaining to USMS FOIA Request number 2020USMS35496.

Should you have any questions you can email usms.foia@usdoj.gov<mailto:usms.foia@usdoj.gov>.

Thank you,

USMS FOIA Administrative Team

title: Merced County Probation: Graffiti Abatement Service Request Form Photographs

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Merced County Probation: Graffiti Abatement Service Request Form Photographs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs uploaded via the Merced County Probation Department's Graffiti Abatement Service Request Form website:

https://www.countyofmerced.com/forms.aspx?fid=125

This is just a request for the images uploaded, not for the identifying information in the form itself. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the Merced County Probation department. 

Other municipalities in California publicly display reported graffiti violations online. Thank you for the Merced County Probation Office's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Use of force data (2017-2024) (Tempe Police Department)

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Use of force data (2017-2024) (Tempe Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2024. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Surprise AZ Planning & Zoning, ICE Warehouse

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Surprise AZ Planning & Zoning, ICE Warehouse
communications.communication:
To the Public Records/FOIA Officer:
Under the Arizona Public Records Act, I am requesting an opportunity to obtain copies of public records relating to the property located at 13290 W Sweetwater Ave, Surprise, AZ 85379.
Specifically, I am requesting the following records created, submitted, or received between January 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed:

- All building permits, renovation permits, variance requests, or "Change of Use" applications filed for the aforementioned property.
- All code compliance inspection reports, certificates of occupancy, and violation notices regarding the property.
- Any site plans, architectural drawings, or engineering evaluations submitted by the property owner, their LLC, or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) regarding retrofitting the facility.

To expedite this request and reduce the administrative burden on your office, I am requesting these records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF for documents, .PST or .EML for emails) delivered via email or secure download link.

If any part of this request is denied or redacted, please provide a written response citing the specific statutory exemption being applied.

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me beforehand if the estimated cost will exceed $25. However, I also request a waiver of all fees, as the disclosure of this information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of local government operations and infrastructure planning.

Thank you for your prompt assistance and public service.

Sincerely,
Matthew Ruehle

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Orange County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Orange County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: [Records Center] SHERIFF FOIL REQUEST :: P002450-012926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: SHERIFF FOIL REQUEST of January 29, 2026., Reference # P002450-012926. 
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe, 
Orange County received a FOIL request from you on January 29, 2026. Your request mentioned: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2201994/" Orange County has reviewed your request and has determined that some of the records requested, or parts of the records requested, are exempt from disclosure under FOIL for the following reasons: NY Public Officers Law §87 (2)(b); §89 (2)(b) – Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy 
 
  * Removal of Street Address 
  * Simplification (undetailed) of Medical Information. 
 NY Public Officers Law §89 (3)(a) – Request does not reasonably describe the records sought (records cannot be identified or retrieved with reasonable effort given the agency’s filing system) 
 
  * Prior to October of 2023, the Orange County Sheriff’s Office did not have a computer program with the capacity to extract and compile the statistical information you seek. Paper records prior to October 2023, are not indexed or maintained in a manner that permits the Sheriff’s Office to identify and retrieve the requested documents with reasonable effort. Locating such records would be tantamount to searching for a needle in a haystack, a task that Freedom of Information Law does not require an agency to undertake. Your request for records from 2017 - October 2023 is denied, as it does not “reasonably describe” the records sought. See Committee on Open Government FOIL Advisory Opinion 17989 (February 3, 2010) (“the agency is not required to search the haystack, even though it is known that needles are there, somewhere.)” Responsive non-exempt records and/or redacted records have been made available. Please log in to the FOIL Records Center at the following link to retrieve the responsive documents. SHERIFF FOIL REQUEST - P002450-012926 
Sincerely, 
Deputy Schmitt #247 
Orange County Sheriff's Office We do not waive other grounds of exemption by limiting our response to the above and reserve the right to assert such if this matter is appealed.  Should you wish to appeal this determination, you must do so in writing submitted within thirty (30) days of the date of this letter to the attention of: Richard B. Golden, County Attorney, FOIL Appeals Officer, Orange County Department of Law, Government Center, 255-275 Main Street, Goshen, New York 10924.

title: Financial Report Request (Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Financial Report Request (Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

communications.subject: FOIA 33704   RE: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Horry County Police Department)
communications.communication:
Mr. Sinyangwe,

Following up with your FOIA request (attached) it is anticipated that the actual cost of providing the records requested will amount to approximately $39,175.00. Please forward to us the amount of $9,793.75 [25% deposit] and, when received, we will begin the process of compiling records. Please remit payment as set forth above by cash, certified funds, company check or money order (made payable to Horry County Government). If costs should end up being less than the amount deposited, you will be refunded the difference. Should costs end up being more, you will need to pay the difference at or prior to the time of delivery.

Should we not receive your deposit within twenty (20) calendar days from the date of this correspondence, we will consider your request closed. Online and over the phone payments are not available at this time.

Please remit payment to:

Horry County Government
Public Information
P.O. Box 1236
c/o Aaron Spelbring
Conway, SC 29528

Due to the volume of records requested, it will likely take a significant amount of time to fulfill this request, over and above the time set forth in the SC FOIA for production. If you should not so advise, we will assume that you consent to such extension.

Thank you,

Aaron P. Spelbring | FOIA Manager
Horry County Government
Public Information Office
211 Beaty St., Conway, South Carolina 29526
Tel: (843) 915-8424 | Fax: (843) 915-6390 | Spelbring.Aaron@horrycountysc.gov<mailto:Spelbring.Aaron@horrycountysc.gov>
www.horrycountysc.gov<http://www.horrycountysc.gov/>

**Note – Our physical office address has changed. Please update your records accordingly if applicable**

All e-mail correspondence to and from this address may be subject to public disclosure under the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act (FOIA). This correspondence is intended exclusively for the individual or entity to which it is addressed and may contain information that is proprietary, privileged, confidential or otherwise legally exempt from disclosure.

In accordance with South Carolina law, any personal information (including, but not limited to, a person’s name, date of birth, social security number, home address, home telephone number, driver’s identification number, medical or disability information, financial information, etc...) obtained through the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act cannot be used for commercial solicitation purposes directed to any person in this State. Anyone violating this provision is guilty of a misdemeanor, and upon conviction, may be fined up to $500, imprisoned up to one year, or both.

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Iowa Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Iowa Open Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Iowa Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: FOIA Request – October 27, 2024 Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – October 27, 2024 Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

February 21, 2026

FOIA Officer Regional Center for Animal Care & Protection (RCACP)
Melinda Rector
mrector@roanokecountyva.gov

Re: FOIA Request – Custody, Care, and Handling of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente), October 27, 2024

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I request records related to the custody, handling, and care of a black-and-white tuxedo cat named Valiente, rescued on October 27, 2024 at Towers Shopping Center, Roanoke, Virginia. This request is limited to records held by RCACP.

Please provide all records, electronic or paper, in the following categories:

1. Personnel / Custody Information
Names and positions of RCACP staff who handled, evaluated, or had custody of Valiente after the rescue.
Records identifying any staff who received, transferred, or otherwise maintained custody of the cat.

2. Intake / Care / Transfer Records
Intake logs, stray reports, kennel or holding records, and any custody forms for Valiente on or about October 27–30, 2024.
Records reflecting transfer to RCACP from Police, Fire-EMS, or other entities.
Records of veterinary care, evaluation, or treatment performed while the cat was in RCACP custody.
Records reflecting release, adoption, euthanasia, or continued custody.

3. Communications
Emails, memoranda, or other written communications referencing Valiente.
Communications between RCACP and Police, Fire-EMS, veterinary providers, or other entities regarding the cat.

4. Metadata / Additional Notes
If records exist electronically, please provide metadata showing creation, modification, or access, to the extent easily available.
Please first confirm which records exist and provide an estimate of any fees before producing records, so that I may consider narrowing the request if necessary.
If portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please identify the statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable portions.

Please note that communications with me around the October 27, 2024 incident may have used a different phone number than the one listed above; records associated with that number are responsive.

Thank you for your assistance in fulfilling this request.
Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr. 

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se
1328 Peachtree Street NE
Atlanta, Georgia 30309-3209
(404) 389-5642
csdelozier@pm.me

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr..

Mr. Nash/Mr. DeLozier - 

Attached please find email correspondence between Mr. DeLozier and RCACP regarding the report of his missing cat.  Unfortunately, we did not intake Valiente into the shelter system.  There are no other documents available from the Regional Center for Animal Care and Protection (RCACP).  I have reviewed both our shelter software for that time period as well as physical files; and there are no documents other than the emails from and to Mr. DeLozier that I have found that reference a  black/white Tuxedo female cat found in the general area of Towers Mall in Roanoke, Virginia. We are the municipal shelter for the City of Roanoke, Counties of Botetourt and Roanoke, and the Town of Vinton.  We do not have any authority regarding Animal Control efforts from the Animal Control Divisions of those respective agencies.  We are simply where the animals come to be housed for legal stray holds.  If you want specific reports from the City of Roanoke Police Department Animal Control Division, you would need to contact that agency directly.  I hope this information is helpful.

Sincerely,
Melinda W. Rector
Director of Operations, RCACP

title: BRFSS Illinois

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: BRFSS Illinois
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Copies of the questionnaires distributed by Illinois DPH as part of the process of collecting data for the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System in 2024, including the questionnaires for optional modules. I am particularly interested in the questionnaire for the optional module on Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity (SOGI), but would like copies of the questionnaires for all optional modules administered by Illinois DPH that year.
2. Aggregated statistics of the data submitted by Illinois DPH to the CDC as part of the BRFSS data collection process from the SOGI module and any questions from the broader BRFSS survey that were broken down by gender (M/F/other). I do not need individual response level data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lena Bohman

communications.subject: Illinois Department of Public Health Freedom of Information Act Request No. DPH 26-0301 FOIA
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor,

This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act request for the following: 1. Copies of the questionnaires distributed by Illinois DPH as part of the process of collecting data for the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System in 2024, including the questionnaires for optional modules. I am particularly interested in the questionnaire for the optional module on Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity (SOGI) but would like copies of the questionnaires for all optional modules administered by Illinois DPH that year. 2. Aggregated statistics of the data submitted by Illinois DPH to the CDC as part of the BRFSS data collection process from the SOGI module and any questions from the broader BRFSS survey that were broken down by gender (M/F/other). I do not need individual response level data.

You can download the information you are seeking from the following website: https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp. Questionnaires going back to 1998 are on the left dropdown. Summary data and demographic crosstabs are available by selecting a year and a topic.  The specific links to 2024<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/ques/2024IllinoisBRFSSquestionnaire.pdf> Questionnaire SOGI: Summary<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?selTopic=_SEXOR&area=il&yr=2024&form=strata&show=freq>, Male Sexual Orientation<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?xtabFile=_SEXOR_SOMALE&area=il&yr=2024&selTopic=_SEXOR&form=strata&show=xtab>, Female Sexual Orientation<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?xtabFile=_SEXOR_SOFEMALE&area=il&yr=2024&selTopic=_SEXOR&form=strata&show=xtab>, Gender Identity<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?xtabFile=_SEXOR_TRNSGNDR2&area=il&yr=2024&selTopic=_SEXOR&form=strata&show=xtab>.

Sincerely,
Patricia C. Montalvo
Patricia C. Montalvo
Assistant General Counsel & FOIA Officer
Division of Legal Services
Illinois Department of Public Health
115 South LaSalle Street, 7th Floor
Chicago, IL 60603





State of Illinois - CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: The information contained in this communication is confidential, may be attorney-client privileged or attorney work product, may constitute inside information or internal deliberative staff communication, and is intended only for the use of the addressee. Unauthorized use, disclosure or copying of this communication or any part thereof is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by return e-mail and destroy this communication and all copies thereof, including all attachments. Receipt by an unintended recipient does not waive attorney-client privilege, attorney work product privilege, or any other exemption from disclosure.

George Smith

Dear George Smith, Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles. In accordance with § 119.07(1)(c), Fla. Stat., receipt of your public records request is hereby acknowledged. You substantially seek the following records: "I hereby request the following records: 
I am writing to formally request access to video footage from a police intervention, specifically from the body camera worn by the officer(s) and the dashcam of the police vehicles involved. 
Exact date of incident: October 20, 2024 
Names of parties involved: Margot Whisant McDaniels." title: UAS Deployments 2025 (Renton Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: UAS Deployments 2025 (Renton Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

February 20, 2026 

RE: Open Records Request of January 05, 2026, Reference # P000139-010526 

Dear Rose Terse, 

The City of Renton Police Department received an open records request from you on January 05, 2026.

"On January 12, 2026, a 5-day letter was sent to you with an anticipated response date of February 24, 2026. 

You requested the following
Other - (Provide description below)
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2170453/


City Response
After a diligent search, the Renton Police Department has located records responsive to your request. The following record has been provided: An Excel Spreadsheet documenting drone deployments (flights) from 01/01/2025-12/31/2025 and is provided without exemption or redaction.
 
Please note: Commander Karlewicz advised we do not track purpose of each deployment in the flight logs. 

The record has been released to our online City of Renton's Public Records Center. You can log into the system and download your responsive records.

Police - Public Records Request - P000139-010526

With the records provided and referenced above, the City believes that this fulfills and closes your public records request (for retention and statute of limitations); no further action will be taken on request #P000139-010526.  If you have questions or concerns regarding the records provided in response to this request, please let us know within 10 business days.  Thank you.

Sincerely, 


Jennifer Hermann, CPRO
Staff Services Supervisor
Renton Police Department
425-430-7635"

title: Videotape Recording of Composite Sketch Creation

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Videotape Recording of Composite Sketch Creation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Indicating what happened to the videotape recording of the creation of the composite sketch by Karl Nollsch that occurred on 2/3/1978. There is documentation of its existence and has been viewed by detectives after the 2001 DNA connection was made between the East Area Rapist attacks in Northern CA and the Original Night Stalker rape/murders in Southern CA. This is evidence in a double homicide so it existed as late as 2018. I am requesting any documentation of its existence and, if it no longer exists, what happened to it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Neville

title: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – 20,287.4 GB of data - Declaration of Matt Gillingham (10-13-2025)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – 20,287.4 GB of data - Declaration of Matt Gillingham (10-13-2025)
communications.communication:
LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – 20,287.4 GB of data - Declaration of Matt Gillingham (10-13-2025)

Via email: publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org

Date: October 15, 2025

Scope and Records Requested

Please produce, in native electronic format, the complete set of electronic records that comprise the “20,287.4 GB of data” referenced in the Declaration of Matt Gillingham (filed October 13, 2025) -  copy attached. Produce the data exactly as kept—preserving original file types, filenames, directory structure, timestamps, and embedded metadata. 

- Deliver by direct email or direct HTTPS links that do not require registration, passwords, expiring security keys, or third-party portals. RCW 42.56.080(1) requires honoring requests by email; use of any portal is optional for the requester.

- Provide rolling installments as records are ready.

Delivery Method Requested: Direct email and/or direct HTTPS download links (no account creation, passwords, or expiring “security keys”).

Exemptions & Redactions

If asserting any exemption (RCW 42.56.210 et seq.):

- Produce a specific exemption log identifying each record/portion withheld, the precise exemption, and a brief explanation.

- Redact only exempt portions; produce all non-exempt portions; mark redactions with clear labels (WAC 44-14-04003/-04004). Include record metadata where it is itself a public record.

Fees, Bundling, and Estimates

- Please provide, in advance, a written total fee estimate itemizing the statutory basis and anticipated charges (including any per-GB and per-file amounts) as required under RCW 42.56.120, before incurring any costs.

- For electronic delivery, limit charges to those authorized by statute. To avoid artificially inflating fees, bundle responsive records into the fewest practical electronic files (e.g., a single .zip per installment) before calculating any “per-file” counts or transmission-size charges.

- Propose cost-reducing alternatives (single compressed archives, batched email, consolidated downloads) if costs may be significant.

Timeliness, Assistance, and Clarification

- Please acknowledge and provide a reasonable time estimate and installment plan (RCW 42.56.520).

- Provide the fullest assistance (RCW 42.56.100; WAC 44-14-04003).

- If any portion seems unclear, identify the specific uncertainty and propose practical scope/keyword/custodian refinements; I will respond promptly.

Preservation - Anticipate Litigation

Preserve all potentially responsive records while this request is pending (RCW 42.56.100; WAC 44-14-03005). If any responsive records were destroyed pursuant to retention schedules, state the schedule citation, destruction date, and any available secondary copies or logs.

Authentication

Identify any records produced in a redacted or partial form; indicate if any records are produced as best available copies due to system constraints. If any systems cannot export in native format, briefly explain the limitation.

Please send all correspondence and productions to this email. Thank you.

attachment: Declaration of Matt Gillingham October 13, 2025

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – 20,287.4 GB of data - Declaration of Matt Gillingham (10-13-2025)
communications.communication:
Dear Requester,
We noticed that your message did not reference Washington’s Public Records Act (RCW 42.56), which governs how public records requests must be submitted and processed. Are you submitting a public records request?

If so, please use the Lake Washington School District’s official process. You can submit your request by visiting https://www.lwsd.org/services/public-records-request . This ensures your inquiry is properly routed and tracked.

Under RCW 42.56, requesters are legally required to provide sufficient detail to allow the agency to identify the records being requested. A submission that lacks a reasonably clear description of the records or information being sought does not meet the requirements of RCW 42.56 and cannot be processed as a valid public records request.

To help us assist you efficiently, please clarify your request by identifying the specific records or facts you are seeking. Once submitted through the proper channel with adequate detail, we’ll be happy to respond in accordance with the law.

If you need further clarification or assistance with the submission process, feel free to contact us directly.

Sincerely,

Redmond, WA 98052

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2025-272
communications.communication:
via email to PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – 20,287.4 GB of data -  Declaration of Matt Gillingham (10132025)

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST RCW 42.56

To whom it may concern,

I request the following identifiable public records to be produced:

1.	Please produce, in native electronic format, the complete set of electronic records that comprise the “20,287.4 GB of data” referenced in the Declaration of Matt Gillingham (filed October 13, 2025) – a copy of Mr. Gillingham’s declaration is attached. The public records I am requesting are identified on Page 6, Paragraph 27, of the Gillingham declaration:

“27. Notably, 31% of current requesters are using the Muckrock platform, yet those requests account for 95% of the total data volume, representing 20,287.4 GB of data. This disproportionate data load significantly impacts the time and resources required for review, redaction, and fulfillment.”

I request a complete electronic copy of the “20,287.4 GB of data” identified and referenced by Superintendent Matt Gillingham on Page 6, Paragraph 27, of the DECLARATION OF MATT GILLINGHAM IN SUPPORT OFDEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT REGARDING THE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT, CH. 42.56 RCW, dated October 13, 2025. Copy attached. 
 
RECORDS INSTALLMENTS  ORDER OF PRODUCTION

If production of responsive records will be completed in multiple installments, please begin by producing records in REVERSE CHRONOLOGICAL FORMAT BY DATE. The most recent public records should be produced first and the oldest records produced last by date.
RECORDS INSTALLMENTS  METHOD OF PRODUCTION

Deliver by direct email or direct HTTPS links that do not require registration, passwords, expiring security keys, or third-party portals. RCW 42.56.080(1) requires honoring requests by email; use of any portal is optional for the requester.

Provide rolling installments as records are ready.

Delivery Method Requested: Direct email and/or direct HTTPS download links (no account creation, passwords, or expiring “security keys”).

EXEMPTIONS & REDACTIONS

If asserting any exemption (RCW 42.56.210 et seq.):

Produce a specific exemption log identifying each record/portion withheld, the precise exemption, and a brief explanation.

Redact only exempt portions; produce all nonexempt portions; mark redactions with clear labels (WAC 441404003/04004). Include record metadata where it is itself a public record.

FEES, BUNDLING, AND ESTIMATES

 Please provide, in advance, a written total fee estimate itemizing the statutory basis and anticipated charges (including any per GB and per file amounts) as required under RCW 42.56.120, before incurring any costs. For electronic delivery, limit charges to those authorized by statute. To avoid artificially inflating fees, bundle responsive records into the fewest practical electronic files (e.g., a single .zip per installment) before calculating any “per file” counts or transmission size charges. Propose cost reducing alternatives (single compressed archives, batched email, consolidated downloads) if costs may be significant.

TIMELINESS, ASSISTANCE, AND CLARIFICATION

Please acknowledge and provide a reasonable time estimate and installment plan (RCW 42.56.520)

Provide the fullest assistance (RCW 42.56.100; WAC 441404003). If any portion seems unclear, identify the specific uncertainty and propose practical scope/keyword/custodian refinements; I will respond promptly.

PRESERVATION ANTICIPATE LITIGATION

Preserve all potentially responsive records while this request is pending (RCW 42.56.100; WAC 441403005). If any responsive records were destroyed pursuant to retention schedules, state the schedule citation, destruction date, and any available secondary copies or logs.

AUTHENTICATION

Identify any records produced in a redacted or partial form; indicate if any records are produced as best available copies due to system constraints. If any systems cannot export in native format, briefly explain the limitation.

Please send all correspondence and productions to this email.

Good luck.

communications.subject: Lake Washington School DIstrict, WA. Fee issued notification for PRR-2025-272
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 This email concerns the public records request, Request Number: PRR-2025-272 , you submitted that was received by Lake Washington School District on Tuesday, October 21, 2025. A fee has been issued for your request for records. 
  
 Following the submission of your request, a preliminary search was completed. The estimated volume of records and associated costs are detailed in the invoice provided. District policy requires that you be provided with a summary of applicable anticipated charges before charges are imposed. This is to allow the requestor to revise the request in an effort to reduce the applicable charges. To this end, a detailed invoice is provided separately for your review. Please click here to view: Request Number: PRR-2025-272 
  
 Please be advised that District policy/procedure [https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sdDT6VWnYPCfrNw8GE28dNLFru-2BD5tK9Zn73Cfsmpb5xfQNNDYtr1dMm5vktmeCj0iSrGxGsTFS4GDzVEhwn74I-3DIY8K_7kpuqtTJf5oZkMLp1npTECtRCLq8w5jywvveMAUDT-2Be9DA0k9NBdJmzLCdYV4TL0DoZyum8OwDuHbriHPdgduza9adTeRzbe9zjdZWqtCsJwO-2Bn7LHLTcadMA1dwkqz8Chz0N3jgsv-2FCShEkHNKYWDigL1YCaKZNtnMPGRbEYysJC0iBMzG-2ByGYLoem2fAzBQq7CL1BTwTr4cGS5Lk7uisJDIQoJjwbUafSjpmHwTih6WpTrAYvSd7JW-2Bk4nBldg8YOYQEex9FKUXMOEXSD7MrBsMj8qOKTbBo0FSk93ByWKNIQf4ZKetuVJ9XNOr4nY ] contains the following schedule of charges that may apply to your request: 
  
 1. Ten cents per page for public records scanned into an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to scan the records;
2. Five cents per each four electronic files or attachments uploaded to email, cloud-based data storage service, or other means of electronic delivery;
3. Ten cents per gigabyte for the transmission of public records in an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to send the records electronically; and
4. The actual cost of any digital storage media or device provided by the District, the actual cost of any container or envelope used to mail the copies to the requestor, and the actual postage or delivery charge. 
  
 Alternatively, if the District determines and documents that the fees allowed under this procedure are clearly equal to, or less, than two dollars, the District may instead charge a flat fee of two dollars to provide the records. If the District charges a flat fee for the first installment, the District will not charge an additional flat fee or a per page fee for any subsequent installments. 
  
 Due to the volume of records, the District is exercising its discretion under District procedure and RCW 42.56.120(4) to require a deposit of ten percent of the estimated charges associated with this request.  
  
 After payment is received, we will be authorized by District procedure and the Public Records Act to begin reviewing the responsive records. At that point, we will provide you with the anticipated date of an initial installment. 
  
 Please be advised that we are required to allow you a 30-day time frame to make the pre-payment as outlined above, measured from the date of this email. A failure to receive such payment from a requester within that time frame permits us to consider their request abandoned and close it. Also, the District would also be amenable to narrowing the scope of your request in an effort to reduce the associated fees the District intends to assess. 
  
 Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns about the foregoing or any aspect of the District's work to fulfill your request. 
  
 To view your request status, please click Request Number: PRR-2025-272 
  
 

Thank you, 
  
 Public Records Office 
 15212 NE 95th Street
Redmond, WA 98052 
 publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org

communications.subject: PRR-2025-272: responsive records ready
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 Your records request asks for "the complete set of electronic records that comprise the '20,287.4 GB of data' referenced in the Declaration of Matt Gillingham (filed October 13, 2025)." The referenced 20,287.4 GB of data referenced was based on an internal assessment of overall data volume associated with pending requests at that point in time and does not represent a distinct set of culled records that can be reproduced in response to your request. 
  
 Some of the records requests that were used as the basis of the estimate of data volume may now be complete and records produced in response to those requests are provided as responsive records, however the records produced do not equal the data volume referenced in the declaration. 
  
 The responsive records provided are associated with 10 separate public records requests. A summary is attached for your reference that includes document counts, request details, date submitted, and date closed. 
  
 To access the responsive records please use the link and security key provided below: 
  
 Request link: Request Number: PRR-2025-272 
  
 Security key: 3FB8CE7F 
  
 Within the requests associated with the internal assessment, some were closed due to lack of payment or abandonment and no responsive records were provided. 
  
 Additionally, 34 records have been identified and withheld under the following exemptions. 
  
 Please note that under the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (“FERPA”), 20 U.S.C. § 1232g, and its federal implementing regulations, 34 C.F.R. § 99.1 et seq., are “other statute[s]” per RCW 42.56.070 that exempt or prohibit disclosure of specific records. 
  
 In addition, RCW 28A.605.030 provides that a school may not release students’ education records except as authorized by FERPA or RCW 28A.600.475. 
  
 Further, RCW 42.56.230(2)(iii) exempts personal information for a student in any records pertaining to the student. RCW 42.56.230 findings intents are as follows: 
  
 Findings—Intent—2023 c 182: "The legislature recognizes the important public interest in maintaining an open and transparent government and the critically important role that our public records act plays in ensuring the people of Washington have access to public records maintained by state and local agencies with narrow exemptions. The legislature recognizes that protecting the personal information of our minor students is also of critical importance, especially when disclosure of that personal information could potentially jeopardize the physical or mental health and safety of a student. While current state law also acknowledges the importance of protecting a student's privacy by providing an exemption for personal information in records maintained on a student's behalf, the legislature notes that there have been instances where information has been released from records held by schools that contains personal information about a student or related to a student and the release allows the student to be identified. The legislature intends to provide additional clarity in this narrow situation involving the disclosure of personal information related to a student that may be found in a nonstudent record. This clarity is intended to ensure school districts understand that they have the ability to protect a student's privacy and redact personal information related to a student regardless of the type of record that the information is found in." [ 2023 c 182 s 1.] 
  
 Consistent with the requirements of the Public Records Act and FERPA for protecting student privacy, 19 records were identified as exempt from disclosure pursuant to RCW 42.56.230 1, 2(a), 2(iv), and FERPA. 
  
 Additionally, the District reviewed the responsive records to identify information relating to District staff that is exempt from disclosure. As a result of this review, 15 records were identified as containing employee personal information that is unrelated to the conduct of government or the performance of official duties and have been withheld or redacted pursuant to RCW 42.56.230(3) and RCW 42.56.250, as applicable. Further, information was withheld where disclosure would constitute an unreasonable invasion of personal privacy under RCW 42.56.050. These redactions were applied narrowly and only to the extent necessary to protect staff privacy, and all non-exempt portions of responsive records have been released in accordance with RCW 42.56.070(1). 
  
 If you have any questions about the records, please contact us as soon as possible. If you have questions about public records requests, additional information is available on our website at https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2saf7hzZRTWl6NaNk2Ngw2xI94WCmlOAuubZY2eGJvWNCDbiKW-2FoHs-2Fp1FxW-2Fre9NBQ-3D-3D89YZ_7kpuqtTJf5oZkMLp1npTECtRCLq8w5jywvveMAUDT-2Be9DA0k9NBdJmzLCdYV4TL0DoZyum8OwDuHbriHPdgduza9adTeRzbe9zjdZWqtCsL3DVW7aPavAmbpIoBcEEZpTnFDQFPrBBByBypG7pEwxzZg3P4MvsHJGPzgZPOCpsJWsRKwEEw-2BJHrUYSFSHjesPXbVzYUUc9LJKHjDFI5Wdgbnf3LyFPuopNiUOIhwqQQzJzCdIbrnAO-2BGmc6ft9hUv5MQDTd-2BRSDg-2B3o0Apot-2Fk5UpHB0W-2F6sSDsbah29-2BKfGh0h-2ByvabQMRJgNXRuusS ; . 
  
 Thank you, 
  
 Public Records Office 
  
 15212 NE 95th Street 
  
 Redmond, Washington 98052

title: List Of Applications and Department Partners Using Login.Gov For Identity Management

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: List Of Applications and Department Partners Using Login.Gov For Identity Management
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of applications and department partners currently using login.gov in production for identity management, applications and partners that have communicated to GSA or the Login.gov project the intent to use login.gov as an identity provider but are not yet in production (ie still in development, testing, QA, etc), and any publicly available URLs where this information is already available on partners.login.gov.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brandon Galbraith

Dear Brandon Marchman : 
This correspondence acknowledges your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request received by the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) on November 02, 2023. Your request number is 2024-FOI-00101. Please refer to this number in all future correspondence concerning this request. 
Your request seeks:  A list of applications and department partners currently using login.gov in production for identity management, applications and partners that have communicated to GSA or the Login.gov project the intent to use login.gov as an identity provider but are not yet in production (ie still in development, testing, QA, etc), and any publicly available URLs where this information is already available on partners.login.gov. As your request has been assigned to the Simple track, GSA has twenty (20) business days in which to respond (excluding weekends and federal holidays). Please note, we may not always be able to provide the documents within this time period. In some cases, we may take an extension and will advise you. Your requester category is Other. Please be advised that your request may be subject to fees for search, review, and/or duplication costs. Should this be the case, you will be given an estimate of the costs and further instructions. GSA charges $49.00 an hour for search and review time. All estimates are developed in good faith and are based on our reasonable judgment. However, due to the unique nature of each request and complexity of documents involved, actual costs to search and review the material may vary. You will be notified of fees, time frames, and any other issues prior to and throughout the process. 
You may access your original request and review the status at pal.gsa.gov. You may also communicate with GSA’s FOIA Requester Service Center through that link. communications.subject: GSA FOIA: Response to Your Status Inquiry
communications.communication:
Dear Brandon Marchman: 
This is in response to your status inquiry received on March 13, 2025, for FOIA request number 2024-FOI-00101 which was received by the GSA Requester Service Center on November 02, 2023. 
To the greatest extent practicable, GSA responds to FOIA requests by order of receipt. Your current request status is Under final review by program counsel. This means responsive records have been collected and are under review. Please note that GSA averages 53 days to respond to a request on the simple simple track and 146 days to respond to a request on the complex track. 

If you haveany questions or concerns, y ou may contact the GSA FOIA Public Liaison, David Eby at (202) 213-2745 or by email at david.eby@gsa.gov to discuss your FOIA request. . Thank you for your continued patience as we work to processyour FOIA request. 

Respectfully, 

Freedom of Information Act Requester ServiceCenter 
U.S General Service Administration

title: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Under the Ohio Open Records Law, §149.43 et seq., I hereby request the following records:

Copies of records submitted by the Verdantas civil engineering firm regarding utilities serving the Arsenal-1 Hyperscale Manufacturing Facility in Pickaway County. Specifically, I am seeking all applications and construction plans for an extension of a water line and sanitary/sewer systems to serve the Anduril plant, including a proposed timeline for construction.

At a Madison Township Trustees zoning hearing in December, Amanda Spencer, the director of land development at Verdantas, described the planned utilities, saying: “As far as utilities go, we are planning an extension of a water line that will run along the roadway. It is tied in to an existing water line that was constructed with previous phases of development. It will head north on Airbase road, and it will head east on Airbase road. Eventually, it will continue south on Walnut Creek Pike and end at the southeast corner of the development. The sanitary will tie into a long planned sanitary extension by the City of Columbus that will be constructed on the south side of Airbase road, right along the top of this development.”

I first submitted this request to the Pickaway County Engineer, whose office told me to direct records requests related to water lines serving the plant to the Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I would request a prompt response to this request.  If you expect a significant delay in responding to or in fulfilling this request, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies of the requested records.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes, Inkstick Media

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

Below is a recent request from your organization we received on Sunday  January 18, 2026. Earnhart is supplying potable water to the subject project. The City of Columbus is taking the sanitary sewer (copied on this reply). To date, we have not received water line plans from Verdantas for their proposed public water line work along Airbase Rd. We have, however, attached a related public water line project that will eventually connect to the Verdantas project that you cite. These plans are in draft form and are not to be used for bidding or construction.


Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District
Ohio Open Records Law Office
P.O. Box 151
2030 Stoneridge Drive
Circleville, OH 43113

January 18, 2026

title: Information on Flock Saftey and AI-assisted Cameras

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Information on Flock Saftey and AI-assisted Cameras
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- The location of all Flock Safety or other AI-assisted cameras installed in the last 90 days (please share locations as map coordinates or a street address with nearest cross-street)
- The number of Flock Safety or other AI-assisted cameras planned to be installed and the location where DPS plans to put them (please share locations as map coordinates or a street adress with nearest cross-street)
- All email communications with Immigration and Customs Enforcement regarding data sharing from Flock Safety cameras 
- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety automated license plate readers (ALPRs) over the last 30 days
- The names of the hot lists/watch lists used with Flock Safety automated license plate readers
- The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- All agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
- All contracts, MOUs, or other agreements with Flock Safety
- All procurement documents related to Flock Safety

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen

communications.subject: Welcome to the Public Information Request Portal!
communications.communication:
Dear Concerned  Citizen, 
 
Thank you for registering with the Texas Department of Public Safety Public Information Request Portal.

Texas Department of Public Safety

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of February 04, 2026., Reference # P003182-020426. 
Dear Concerned Citizen, 
The Texas Department of Public Safety received a public information request from you on February 04, 2026. Your request mentioned: "Please See attachment for description." 
Please log into the system to retrieve the information located in response to your 
request. Regarding the parts of your request concerning communications with ICE and agreements 
with HOAs, etc., there are no documents. 
DPS has located other records responsive to your request; however, we believe these records may be excepted from required public disclosure at this time.  DPS is seeking a ruling from the Office of the Attorney General (OAG) with respect to disclosure of these records, and a copy of our request letter is attached.  You will be notified directly by the OAG when a ruling is issued. 
If you have any questions or need additional information, please submit your response through this portal. 
Sincerely, 
Brian Riemenschneider 
Assistant General Counsel 
OGC

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Portage County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Portage County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Officer Disciplinary Records - E Kuznetsov

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Officer Disciplinary Records - E Kuznetsov
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

During an incident on 2/9 involving an ICE vehicle, a Minnetonka Police Officer is seen on video shoving a women while her back is turned. The officer appears to be wearing badge 193, with a nameplate listing "E Kuznetsov". We are seeking information about this officer's training and disciplinary records, including:
1) Complaints filed against the officer from their start date through the date you fulfill this request. Please include substantiated/investigated complaints as well as any there may have been deemed unsubstantiated. We do NOT want any identifying information of the individuals who made the complaints. 
2) All personnel file records referring to disciplinary actions, reprimands, corrective actions, performance evaluations reflecting misconduct, and sustained force findings for Officer E. Kuznetsov.
3) Complete training history for Officer E. Kuznetsov, including course titles, agencies providing training, completion certificates/grades, and dates, specifically: Use-of-Force/De-Escalation training, Crowd Control / Mass Demonstration training, Less-Lethal Weapon / Tactics training, Any annual in-service or refresher training related to force or public order.

Please reach out with any questions, I'm happy to provide clarification if needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

Mr. Segal,

Please see the responses to your request below. If you need additional assistance, please reach out to me via email, jtait@minnetonkamn.gov

1) Complaints filed against the officer from their start date through the date you fulfill this request. Please include substantiated/investigated complaints as well as any there may have been deemed unsubstantiated. We do NOT want any identifying information of the individuals who made the complaints.

We did receive two citizen complaints related to the incident you are describing. These complaints are still in the review process and there is no disposition as of now. Officer Kuznetsov has no other complaints in his file.

2) All personnel file records referring to disciplinary actions, reprimands, corrective actions, performance evaluations reflecting misconduct, and sustained force findings for Officer E. Kuznetsov.

Officer Kuznetsov has no discipline in his file or other materials you have called out. 

3) Complete training history for Officer E. Kuznetsov, including course titles, agencies providing training, completion certificates/grades, and dates, specifically: Use-of-Force/De-Escalation training, Crowd Control / Mass Demonstration training, Less-Lethal Weapon / Tactics training, Any annual in-service or refresher training related to force or public order.

Officer Kuznetsov's training records are attached. If you feel this is not sufficient or if you have questions, please let me know. 

Jason Tait

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Request for Dept. of Correction Commissioners / Wardens Contact Information

communications.subject: Maine Freedom of Access Act Request: Request for Dept. of Correction Commissioners / Wardens Contact Information
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maine Freedom of Access Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of prison wardens and assistant wardens currently employed by the Maine Department of Corrections, including for each record:

- Full names
- Job titles
- Assigned correctional facility
- Government-issued email addresses
- Government-issued phone numbers (direct desk numbers if available, otherwise department phone numbers with or without extensions)
- Department/division

2. A list of state-level commissioners/directors and deputy commissioners/directors currently employed by the Maine Department of Corrections, including for each record:

- Full name
- Official job title
- Division / bureau
- Official work email address
- Official office phone number
- Official work mailing address

If available, I request this information be provided in a machine-readable format such as CSV or Excel.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by 1 M.R.S.A. § 408(6). The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Morgan P Hatch Jr

communications.subject: **FOAA Request** 2025118 Morgan P Hatch Jr 22 Dec 25
communications.communication:
The Maine Department of Corrections has received your request for information under the Maine Freedom of Access Act (FOAA).  Your request will be evaluated to determine if it comes within the FOAA, if it is clear as to what information you are requesting, and if nonconfidential records already exist that would fulfill all or part of your request.  If your request does not come within the FOAA, is not clear, or no records exist, you will be so notified.  If records exist, but they are confidential, you will be notified of the basis for that determination.  If nonconfidential records exist, you will be provided a good faith, nonbinding estimate of the time within which you will be provided the records, as well as an estimate of the costs that are allowed by the FOAA, if applicable.

V/r

Jenna Heald
Maine Department of Corrections
Policy & PowerDMS Assistant
FOAA Coordinator

communications.subject: RE: Maine Freedom of Access Act Request: Request for Dept. of Correction Commissioners / Wardens Contact Information

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Sarasota County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Sarasota County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the FL State Statute 119, Public Information Act. Your request was received in this office on 1/29/2026 and given the reference number S212806-012926 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2202007/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: Florida Public Records Law does not require a governmental body to create new information or documents, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Sarasota County Sheriff's Office
